data.content:
DETR
DETR refers to end-to-end Transformer-based object detection models that eliminate the need for Non-Maximum Suppression (NMS).<SEP>DETR is an end-to-end object detector based on Transformer, proposed by Carion et al., which RT-DETR improves upon.<SEP>DETR is a type of object detection model based on transformers, analyzed in the paper.

RT-DETR
RT-DETR is a real-time end-to-end object detection model proposed in the paper, designed to outperform YOLOs in both speed and accuracy.<SEP>RT-DETR is a real-time object detection model that focuses on reducing computational costs and optimizing query initialization.<SEP>RT-DETR is a real-time end-to-end object detector proposed in the text, designed to improve inference speed and accuracy.<SEP>RT-DETR is an object detection model mentioned in the text.<SEP>RT-DETR is an object detection model represented by red circles in the scatter plot, labeled as "Ours" in the legend.

YOLO
YOLO is a series of real-time object detection frameworks known for their balance between speed and accuracy.<SEP>YOLO is a family of real-time object detection models, discussed in the paper as a baseline for comparison.

Non-Maximum Suppression
Non-Maximum Suppression (NMS) is a post-processing step used in object detection to remove redundant detections.<SEP>Non-Maximum Suppression is a technique used in object detection to remove redundant predictions.

Baidu Inc
Baidu Inc is an organization based in Beijing, China, involved in the research and development of object detection models.

Peking University
Peking University is an educational institution in Shenzhen, China, associated with the research presented in the paper.

COCO
COCO is a dataset used for evaluating object detection models, measuring performance in terms of Average Precision (AP).

Objects365
Objects365 is a large-scale dataset used for pre-training object detection models.<SEP>Objects365 is a pre-training dataset used to improve the performance of RTDETR-R50/R101.<SEP>Objects365 is a large-scale dataset used for pre-training RT-DETR models.

Wenyu Lv
Wenyu Lv is one of the researchers who contributed to the development of RT-DETR.

Yian Zhao
Yian Zhao is one of the researchers who contributed to the development of RT-DETR.

Shangliang Xu
Shangliang Xu is one of the researchers who contributed to the development of RT-DETR.

Guanzhong Wang
Guanzhong Wang is one of the researchers who contributed to the development of RT-DETR.

Jinman Wei
Jinman Wei is one of the researchers who contributed to the development of RT-DETR.

Qingqing Dang
Qingqing Dang is one of the researchers who contributed to the development of RT-DETR.

Yi Liu
Yi Liu is one of the researchers who contributed to the development of RT-DETR.

Jie Chen
Jie Chen is one of the researchers who contributed to the development of RT-DETR.

CNN
CNN refers to Convolutional Neural Networks, which are commonly used in real-time object detection architectures.

Transformer
Transformer is a neural network architecture used in DETRs for object detection.

Real-Time Object Detection
Real-Time Object Detection refers to the ability of a model to detect objects quickly enough for real-time applications.<SEP>Real-Time Object Detection is the goal of RT-DETR, aiming to achieve fast and accurate detection without retraining.

Object Detection
Object detection is the task of identifying and localizing objects within images or videos.<SEP>Object Detection is the task of identifying and locating objects within images or videos.<SEP>Object detection is the task of identifying and localizing objects within an image, which is the focus of the research paper.

T4 GPU
T4 GPU is a type of graphics processing unit used for running and benchmarking object detection models.<SEP>T4 GPU is a graphics processing unit used for testing the speed of NMS operations.<SEP>T4 GPU is a hardware platform on which RT-DETR achieves 108 FPS.

DINO
DINO is another object detection model that RT-DETR outperforms in terms of accuracy and speed.<SEP>DINO is an object detection model discussed in the text.<SEP>DINO is another variant of DETR discussed in the paper's related work section.

Uncertainty-Minimal Query Selection
Uncertainty-Minimal Query Selection is a technique proposed in the paper to improve the quality of initial queries in the decoder.<SEP>Uncertainty-Minimal Query Selection is a technique used in RT-DETR to select high-quality initial queries for the decoder.<SEP>Uncertainty-minimal query selection is a technique used in RT-DETR to improve accuracy.

Hybrid Encoder
Hybrid Encoder is a component of RT-DETR designed to efficiently process multi-scale features.<SEP>Hybrid Encoder is an efficient design introduced in RT-DETR to replace the vanilla Transformer encoder, improving inference speed.

Multi-Scale Features
Multi-Scale Features refer to features extracted at different scales to capture objects of varying sizes.<SEP>Multi-Scale Features are used in RT-DETR to improve inference speed by decoupling intra-scale interaction and cross-scale fusion.

Transformer Encoder
Transformer Encoder is a component of the original Transformer model, which RT-DETR modifies to improve performance.

Real-Time DEtection TRansformer
Real-Time DEtection TRansformer (RT-DETR) is a real-time end-to-end object detection model proposed in the paper, designed to outperform YOLOs in both speed and accuracy.<SEP>Real-Time DEtection TRansformer is the full name of RT-DETR, representing a novel approach to real-time object detection.<SEP>RT-DETR is a proposed real-time object detection model introduced in the paper.

Multi-Layer Decoder Architecture
Multi-Layer Decoder Architecture is a feature of DETR that allows flexible speed tuning in RT-DETR.

YOLO Detectors
YOLO Detectors are previously advanced real-time object detectors that RT-DETR outperforms in both speed and accuracy.

COCO Val2017
COCO Val2017 is a benchmark dataset used for evaluating object detection models.<SEP>COCO Val2017 is a dataset used to evaluate the performance of RT-DETR, achieving 53.1% AP.

X Model
X Model is a previously advanced YOLO detector that RT-DETR outperforms.

YOLO S Model
YOLO S Model is a lighter YOLO detector that scaled RT-DETRs outperform.

L Model
L Model is a previously advanced YOLO detector that RT-DETR outperforms.

YOLO M Model
YOLO M Model is a lighter YOLO detector that scaled RT-DETRs outperform.

DINO-Deformable-DETR-R50
DINO-Deformable-DETR-R50 is a previous detector that RT-DETR-R50 outperforms in accuracy and speed.

YOLOv1
YOLOv1 is the first CNN-based one-stage object detector that achieved true real-time object detection.

Deformable-DETR
Deformable-DETR is a variant of DETR that accelerates training convergence with multi-scale features.<SEP>Deformable-DETR is a variant of DETR discussed in the paper's related work section.

DN-DETR
DN-DETR is a variant of DETR that improves performance through denoising training.

DAB-DETR
DAB-DETR is a variant of DETR that improves performance through iterative refinement.

Efficient DETR
Efficient DETR is a variant of DETR that reduces computational cost by reducing encoder and decoder layers.

Group-DETR
Group-DETR is a variant of DETR that introduces group-wise one-to-many assignment.

Sparse DETR
Sparse DETR is a variant of DETR that reduces computational cost by reducing the number of updated queries.

Lite DETR
Lite DETR is a variant of DETR that enhances encoder efficiency by reducing low-level feature update frequency.

Conditional DETR
Conditional DETR is a variant of DETR that decreases query optimization difficulty.<SEP>Conditional DETR is an object detection model mentioned in the text.

Query Selection
Query Selection is a technique used in two-stage DETR to help initialize queries better.<SEP>Query selection is a challenge in DETRs, addressed by the proposed uncertainty-minimal query selection method.

Anchor DETR
Anchor DETR is a variant of DETR that decreases query optimization difficulty.<SEP>Anchor DETR is an object detection model referenced in the text.

NMS
NMS refers to Non-Maximum Suppression, a technique used in object detection to filter out redundant bounding boxes.<SEP>NMS is a post-processing algorithm used in object detection to eliminate overlapping output boxes by applying confidence and IoU thresholds.<SEP>NMS is a post-processing algorithm used in object detection to eliminate overlapping output boxes.<SEP>NMS stands for Non-Maximum Suppression, a technique used to eliminate overlapping bounding boxes.<SEP>NMS refers to Non-Maximum Suppression, a technique used in object detection to eliminate redundant predictions.

Mixed Query Selection
Mixed Query Selection is a technique suggested by DINO to help better initialize queries.

Confidence Threshold
Confidence Threshold is a parameter in NMS that filters out prediction boxes with scores below a specified value.<SEP>Confidence Threshold is a parameter in NMS that determines the minimum confidence level for box selection.<SEP>Confidence threshold is a parameter used in object detection models to filter detected boxes.<SEP>Confidence threshold is a parameter used in object detection to filter out low-confidence predictions.

End-to-End Speed Benchmark
An end-to-end speed benchmark is established to evaluate the performance of real-time detectors.<SEP>End-to-End Speed Benchmark is a metric established to test the end-to-end inference speed of real-time detectors.

Intra-Scale Interaction
Intra-Scale Interaction refers to the processing of features within the same scale in RT-DETR.

Decoder Layers
Decoder Layers are part of the architecture in RT-DETR that allow flexible speed tuning by adjusting their number.

Cross-Scale Fusion
Cross-Scale Fusion refers to the integration of features from different scales in RT-DETR.

Encoder Layers
Encoder Layers are part of the architecture in RT-DETR that contribute to feature extraction and processing.

Feature Extraction
Feature Extraction is the process of identifying relevant information from input data, used in RT-DETR to improve performance.

Inference Speed
Inference Speed is a key performance metric for real-time object detection, improved by RT-DETR.

Accuracy
Accuracy is a key performance metric for object detection, improved by RT-DETR.

Pre-Training
Pre-Training is a technique used to improve the performance of RT-DETR, using the Objects365 dataset.

NMS Post-Processing
NMS Post-Processing is a step in YOLO detectors that slows down their speed and is eliminated in RT-DETR.

Post-Processing
Post-Processing refers to the steps taken after inference to refine results, such as NMS in YOLO detectors.

Detector
Detector is a general term for object detection models, including YOLO, DETR, and RT-DETR.

One-Stage Object Detector
One-Stage Object Detector is a type of object detection model, exemplified by YOLOv1.

Speed Tuning
Speed Tuning is a feature of RT-DETR that allows adaptation to various real-time scenarios without retraining.<SEP>Speed tuning refers to the flexibility of adjusting the model's speed while maintaining performance.

Anchor-Based
Anchor-Based refers to a category of YOLO detectors that use predefined anchors for object localization.

Two-Stage Object Detector
Two-Stage Object Detector is a type of object detection model, exemplified by some variants of DETR.

Anchor-Free
Anchor-Free refers to a category of YOLO detectors that do not use predefined anchors for object localization.

Overlapping Boxes
Overlapping Boxes are a common issue in YOLO detectors, requiring NMS post-processing to eliminate them.

Hand-Crafted Anchor
Hand-Crafted Anchor is a traditional component in object detection models that DETR eliminates.

Bipartite Matching
Bipartite Matching is a technique used in DETR to directly predict one-to-one object sets.

Iterative Refinement
Iterative Refinement is a technique used in DAB-DETR to improve performance.

Denoising Training
Denoising Training is a technique used in DN-DETR to improve performance.

Group-Wise One-to-Many Assignment
Group-Wise One-to-Many Assignment is a technique used in Group-DETR to improve performance.

Attention Mechanism
Attention Mechanism is a key component in Transformer-based models, enhanced in Deformable-DETR.

Low-Level Features
Low-Level Features are parts of the input data that are processed in the encoder of Lite DETR.

Update Frequency
Update Frequency refers to how often low-level features are updated in Lite DETR.

Interleaved Way
Interleaved Way is a method used in Lite DETR to enhance encoder efficiency.

Figure 1
Figure 1 is a visual representation comparing the performance of RT-DETR with other models.<SEP>Figure 1 is the label for the scatter plot showing the performance comparison of object detection models.

Optimization Difficulty
Optimization Difficulty refers to the challenges in optimizing queries in Conditional DETR and Anchor DETR.

Figure 2
Figure 2 is a visual representation showing the number of boxes at different confidence thresholds.<SEP>Figure 2 is a bar chart comparing the number of boxes for YOLOv5 and YOLOv8 at different confidence thresholds.<SEP>Figure 2 is a bar chart illustrating the number of boxes at different confidence thresholds.

YOLOv5
YOLOv5 is an anchor-based real-time detector used for object detection.<SEP>YOLOv5 is an anchor-based object detection model used for analysis in the context of NMS performance.<SEP>YOLOv5 is an anchor-based object detection model referenced in the image analysis.<SEP>YOLOv5 is an object detection model represented by orange squares in the scatter plot.

Appendix
Appendix contains additional experimental results for RT-DETR.

YOLOv8
YOLOv8 is an anchor-based real-time detector used for object detection.<SEP>YOLOv8 is an anchor-free object detection model used for evaluating accuracy and NMS execution time.<SEP>YOLOv8 is an anchor-free object detection model compared with YOLOv5 in the bar chart.<SEP>YOLOv8 is an object detection model represented by purple inverted triangles in the scatter plot.

COCO val2017
COCO val2017 is a dataset used for testing the accuracy of object detection models.<SEP>COCO val2017 is a dataset used for benchmarking object detection models.

TensorRT FP16
TensorRT FP16 is a precision format used for inference, providing faster computation with reduced numerical precision.

TensorRT EfficientNMSPlugin
TensorRT EfficientNMSPlugin is a specialized NMS implementation that includes multiple kernels for efficient non-maximum suppression.

IoU Threshold
IoU Threshold is a parameter in NMS that determines when two bounding boxes are considered overlapping based on their intersection over union.<SEP>IoU threshold is a parameter used in object detection to determine the overlap between bounding boxes.<SEP>IoU threshold is a parameter used in object detection to determine the overlap between predicted and ground-truth bounding boxes.

End-to-end Speed Benchmark
End-to-end Speed Benchmark is a method for comparing the overall speed of real-time object detectors, including all processing stages.

EfficientNMS Kernel
EfficientNMS Kernel is a specific component of the TensorRT EfficientNMSPlugin responsible for non-maximum suppression.

NMS Execution Time
NMS Execution Time is the time required to perform non-maximum suppression on detected objects.<SEP>NMS execution time is a measure of how long it takes for the Non-Maximum Suppression algorithm to process predictions.

AP
AP refers to Average Precision, a metric used to evaluate the accuracy of object detection models.<SEP>AP stands for Average Precision, a metric used to evaluate the performance of object detection models.

EfficientNMSFilter
EfficientNMSFilter is a kernel within the TensorRT EfficientNMSPlugin used for non-maximum suppression in object detection.

Real-time Detectors
Real-time Detectors are object detection models designed to process images quickly, suitable for applications requiring immediate results.

EfficientNMS
EfficientNMS is a core component of the TensorRT EfficientNMSPlugin responsible for efficiently performing non-maximum suppression.

RadixSort
RadixSort is a sorting algorithm used as part of the TensorRT EfficientNMSPlugin to optimize non-maximum suppression.

YOLOv7
YOLOv7 is an anchor-based real-time detector used for object detection.<SEP>YOLOv7 is an object detection model represented by teal diamonds in the scatter plot.

TensorRT
TensorRT is a deep learning inference platform developed by NVIDIA.<SEP>TensorRT is a deep learning inference platform used for optimizing detector performance.

PP-YOLOE
PP-YOLOE is an anchor-free real-time detector used for object detection.<SEP>PP-YOLOE is an object detection model represented by yellow circles in the scatter plot.

YOLOv6
YOLOv6 is an anchor-based real-time detector used for object detection.

NVIDIA
NVIDIA is a technology company known for its work in GPU and AI development.

EfficientNMSPlugin
EfficientNMSPlugin is a plugin for TensorRT that implements NMS post-processing for YOLO detectors.

I/O
I/O refers to input/output operations, which are excluded from the end-to-end speed benchmark calculation.

MemoryCopy
MemoryCopy refers to memory copy operations, which are excluded from the end-to-end speed benchmark calculation.

Scaled RT-DETRs
Scaled RT-DETRs outperform YOLO S Model.

RT-DETR-R50
RT-DETR-R50 outperforms DINO-Deformable-DETR-R50 in accuracy and speed.<SEP>RT-DETR-R50 is a specific variant of the RT-DETR model, achieving 53.1% AP and 108 FPS.

Decoder
Uncertainty-Minimal Query Selection provides high-quality initial queries to the decoder.

RT-DETR-R101
RT-DETR-R101 is pre-trained on Objects365 to improve performance.

This image presents Figure 1 from the paper 'DETRs Beat YOLOs on Real-time Object Detection'. It is a scatter plot comparing the proposed RT-DETR model against state-of-the-art YOLO variants (v5, v6, v7, v8) and PP-YOLOE. The chart demonstrates that RT-DETR achieves a better trade-off between accuracy (COCO AP) and inference speed (Latency on T4 GPU), effectively outperforming existing real-time detectors by eliminating the need for Non-Maximum Suppression (NMS).

data.entity_name: RT-DETR Paper Page 2 - Related Work and Methodology (image)
data.content: This image shows page 2 of the RT-DETR research paper, detailing the methodology for the proposed Real-Time DEtection TRansformer. It outlines the efficient hybrid encoder and uncertainty-minimal query selection designed to overcome DETR computational bottlenecks. The page includes Figure 2, a bar chart showing box count vs. confidence thresholds, and Table 1, quantifying the impact of NMS hyperparameters on speed and accuracy. It also contains the 'Related Work' section reviewing YOLO and DETR architectures.

data.entity_name: Page 3 of Research Paper on Detector Speed and NMS Analysis (image)
data.content: This image is page 3 of a technical paper analyzing the end-to-end speed of object detectors. It features a bar chart comparing the number of output boxes for YOLOv5 and YOLOv8 at different confidence thresholds, and a table detailing the impact of IoU and confidence thresholds on Average Precision (AP) and Non-Maximum Suppression (NMS) execution time. The accompanying text explains the analysis of NMS hyperparameters and establishes a benchmark for comparing detector speeds.

YOLOv6-v3.0
YOLOv6-v3.0 is an object detection model represented by dark blue stars in the scatter plot.

data.entity_name: DETRs Beat YOLOs on Real-time Object Detection
data.content:
DETRs Beat YOLOs on Real-time Object Detection
This is the title of a research paper comparing the performance of DETRs and YOLOs in real-time object detection.

COCO AP
COCO AP is a metric representing accuracy, measured in percentage, on the y-axis of the scatter plot.

MS COCO Object Detection
MS COCO Object Detection is the title of the scatter plot, visualizing the performance trade-off between accuracy and speed for various object detection models.

End-to-end Latency T4 TensorRT FP16
End-to-end Latency T4 TensorRT FP16 is a metric representing speed or latency, measured in milliseconds, on the x-axis of the scatter plot.

Research Paper
The research paper is a scholarly document presenting findings on real-time object detection models.

Two-column Format
Two-column format is the layout used in the research paper, typically seen in academic publications.

Academic Format
Academic format refers to the standard structure of a research paper, including title, author list, affiliations, abstract, and introduction.

R18 Scaled
R18 Scaled is a specific point on the scatter plot, labeled for reference.

Scatter Plot
A scatter plot is a type of data visualization that displays the relationship between two variables, in this case, accuracy and speed.

R50 Scaled
R50 Scaled is a specific point on the scatter plot, labeled for reference.

R101
R101 is a specific point on the scatter plot, labeled for reference.

R50
R50 is a specific point on the scatter plot, located near 9ms latency and 53% AP, indicating superior performance.

Legend
The legend in the scatter plot distinguishes each model type by color and shape.

Author List
The author list is part of the research paper's standard academic format, listing the contributors to the study.

Abstract
The abstract is part of the research paper's standard academic format, providing a summary of the study's objectives and findings.

Affiliations
Affiliations are part of the research paper's standard academic format, indicating the institutional affiliations of the authors.

Introduction
The introduction is part of the research paper's standard academic format, outlining the background and purpose of the study.

Image Path
The image path is the file location of the research paper's first page, stored in a specific directory.

Vision Pages
Vision Pages is a folder containing images related to the research paper's content.

Knowledge
Knowledge refers to the information and insights presented in the research paper about object detection models.

Page 1
Page 1 is the first page of the research paper, containing key elements such as the title, author list, and introduction.

GitHub
GitHub is a platform where the efficientNMSPlugin repository is hosted.<SEP>GitHub is the platform where the research paper's data and documents are hosted.

Document
Document refers to the research paper's file, which includes the text and visual elements presented in the image.

User
User refers to the individual who accessed and analyzed the research paper's image.

Data
Data refers to the collection of files and information stored in the specified directory.

Tokyo
Tokyo is the host city of the World Athletics Championship.<SEP>Tokyo is not directly mentioned in the text but may be associated with the research paper's context.

DETRs
DETRs refers to Detection Transformers, a type of object detection model discussed in the research paper.

YOLOs
YOLOs are a family of real-time object detection models, contrasted with DETRs in the paper.

Efficient Hybrid Encoder
The efficient hybrid encoder is a technical component of RT-DETR that decouples intra-scale interaction and cross-scale fusion.

World Athletics Championship
The World Athletics Championship is a global sports competition featuring top athletes in track and field.

100m Sprint Record
The 100m sprint record is a benchmark in athletics, recently broken by Noah Carter.

Noah Carter
Noah Carter is a sprinter who set a new 100m sprint record at the World Athletics Championship.

Carbon-Fiber Spikes
Carbon-fiber spikes are advanced sprinting shoes that provide enhanced speed and traction.

World Athletics Federation
The World Athletics Federation is the governing body overseeing the World Athletics Championship and record validations.

Table 1
Table 1 presents data on the effect of IoU and confidence thresholds on accuracy and NMS execution time.<SEP>Table 1 presents numerical data comparing Average Precision (AP) and NMS execution time across different IoU and confidence thresholds.

Average Precision
Average Precision is a metric used to evaluate the performance of object detection models.

Scientific Research Paper
The scientific research paper titled 'DETRs Beat YOLOs on Real-time Object Detection' discusses advancements in object detection models.

Two-Column Academic Format
The two-column academic format is a standard layout used in research papers for text and figures.

Black Serif Text
Black serif text is the font style used in the paper's text, appearing on a white background.

White Background
The white background is the color of the paper's page, contrasting with the black serif text.

Computational Redundancy
Computational redundancy refers to the inefficiency in processing steps within DETRs, which the paper aims to address.

Real-Time End-to-End Object Detector
The paper proposes the first real-time end-to-end object detector, which is a key contribution.

Real-Time Object Detectors
Real-time object detectors are models designed for fast and efficient object detection, discussed in the paper.

End-to-End Object Detectors
End-to-end object detectors are models that perform detection in a single pass, discussed in the paper.

Number of Boxes
Number of boxes refers to the quantity of detected objects, influenced by confidence and IoU thresholds.

efficientNMSPlugin
efficientNMSPlugin is a GitHub repository containing a plugin for efficient NMS implementation.

Execution Time
NMS execution time is a key factor in evaluating the efficiency of object detection models.

Benchmark
COCO val2017 is used as a benchmark for evaluating the speed of object detection models.
